conversation.text: I want a workflow that monitors weather hourly for three cities and alerts me if it gets too hot anywhere.
Which cities, what defines 'too hot', and where should the alert go?
London, New York, Tokyo. Alert if any city is above 30°C. Send the alert via Telegram to chat ID -1001234567890.
I'll use OpenWeatherMap, one HTTP Request per city. Should I log every reading anywhere, or only the hot ones?
Log all of them to Airtable — base ID 'appK2xGfGNOIrl2gT', table ID 'tbl8xK3nP5mQ7rS9W'. Columns: city, temperature, humidity, timestamp.

executionScenarios.name: happy-path
executionScenarios.description: All 3 cities return weather data, one is above 30°C
executionScenarios.dataSetup: London returns: temp=18.5, humidity=72. New York returns: temp=32.1, humidity=65. Tokyo returns: temp=28.3, humidity=80. The Telegram node returns a success response. The Airtable node returns success for each row created.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. All 3 weather API calls succeed. The Telegram alert is sent mentioning New York (32.1°C). All 3 readings are logged to Airtable with city, temperature, humidity, and timestamp.

executionScenarios.name: no-alerts
executionScenarios.description: All cities are below 30°C — no alert needed
executionScenarios.dataSetup: London returns: temp=15.2, humidity=68. New York returns: temp=22.7, humidity=55. Tokyo returns: temp=19.8, humidity=75. The Airtable node returns success for each row created.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. No Telegram alert is sent (all temps below 30°C). All 3 readings are still logged to Airtable.
